title: Delicious Springy Dutch Semolina Pancakes
description: Savory semolina pancakes with a springy texture, featuring pickle and perfect for a nostalgic Dutch meal, this ovo-vegetarian friendly dish is a treat for the senses, bringing back memories of traditional Dutch cuisine.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together semolina, flour, and salt.
In a separate bowl, whisk together eggs, milk, and melted butter.
Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and stir until just combined.
Fold in chopped pickle and parsley.
Let the batter rest for 10 minutes to allow the semolina to absorb the liquid.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat and grease with a small amount of butter.
Using a 1/4 cup measuring cup, scoop the batter into the skillet.
Cook the pancakes for 2-3 minutes, until bubbles appear on the surface and the edges start to dry.
Flip the pancakes and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until golden brown.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
